entities.description: bookrag is a system compared against baselines that achieves state of the art qa performance
table 5 is the location where the comparison of qa performance between bookrag and baselines is shown
m3docvqa is a dataset used to evaluate the exact match performance of bookrag
layout   vanilla is a baseline method that consistently outperforms vanilla rag
vanilla rag is a baseline method that is outperformed by layout   vanilla
tree traverse is a method highlighted for having suboptimal results due to limitations in hierarchical navigation
graphranker is a method highlighted for having suboptimal results due to limitations in graph based reasoning
tree graph bookindex is a component of bookrag that contributes to its superior performance
agent based planning is a component of bookrag that contributes to its superior performance
18 0  is the margin by which bookrag outperforms the top performing baseline in exact match on m3docvqa
qa performance is the specific task being evaluated and compared in the text
exact match is the metric used to measure the performance of bookrag against baselines
hierarchical navigation is a method used by tree traverse that is noted for missing cross sectional context
graph based reasoning is a method used by graphranker that is noted for drifting into irrelevant scopes
context fragmentation is a limitation of existing baselines that bookrag overcomes
static query workflow is a limitation of existing baselines that bookrag overcomes
cross sectional context is information often missed by methods relying solely on hierarchical navigation
irrelevant scopes are areas that methods relying solely on graph based reasoning may drift into
retrieval is the process of finding evidence  which is improved by layout parsing
generation is the process of creating output  which is made accurate by bookrag
queries are inputs that bookrag effectively classifies to configure optimal workflows
workflows are configured by bookrag to ensure precise evidence retrieval and accurate generation
baselines are the three categories of methods against which bookrag is compared
top performing baseline is the specific baseline that bookrag substantially outperforms
existing baselines are the methods that suffer from context fragmentation and static query workflows
